channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] 2026 Legislative Wrap-Up
item.description:
As the 132nd Maine Legislature raced toward adjournment, lawmakers pushed through a wave of major bills in just a matter of days. The final stretch of the session was fast-moving, and decisions were made that will shape Maine’s economy, healthcare system, and tax climate for years to come. Maine Policy Institute closely tracked many of […]

The post 2026 Legislative Wrap-Up appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF in Las Cruces Sun News: Las Cruces overspent. It’s time to cut | Opinion
item.description:
The following appeared in the Las Cruces Sun-News on April 26, 2026. After years of overspending the City of Las Cruces, like Albuquerque, faces a budget deficit.  Unlike Albuquerque which has lost people, Las Cruces continues to grow and has seen a 7.08% increase since the 2020 census. That means that the City can and should be able to […]

The post RGF in Las Cruces Sun News: Las Cruces overspent. It’s time to cut | Opinion appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] Coalition forms to educate Arkansans on dangers of Educational Rights Amendment
[Opportunityarkansas] Two groups leading initiatives to end Arkansas’ income tax
[Alabamapolicy] The Business of Hate: Why Alabama and the Nation Must Reject the SPLC’s Extremism
item.description:
For decades, the Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC) has operated from the Poverty Palace in Montgomery, casting itself as the moral arbiter of American life.

The post The Business of Hate: Why Alabama and the Nation Must Reject the SPLC’s Extremism appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Gambling Breeds Greed and Corruption
item.description:
Campaign money from the sports betting industry is pouring into Alabama ahead of the May 19 primary.

The post Gambling Breeds Greed and Corruption appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] Beacon Poll: May 2026 Results
item.description: Today, we released the latest installment of the Beacon Poll, a statewide survey of 1,200 Tennessee voters featuring questions about voters’ views on energy sources and production in Tennessee, the condition of roads and what revenue sources could be used to improve them, and political approval ratings at the state and federal level.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] STATEMENT FROM JERSEY1ST, NEW JERSEY POLICY INSTITUTE, AND AMERICANS FOR PROSPERITY–NEW JERSEY ON NEW WORKER CLASSIFICATION RULE
item.description:
For Immediate Release: May 6, 2026 TRENTON, N.J. – In response to Governor Sherrill’s adoption of new worker classification regulations tied […]

The post STATEMENT FROM JERSEY1ST, NEW JERSEY POLICY INSTITUTE, AND AMERICANS FOR PROSPERITY–NEW JERSEY ON NEW WORKER CLASSIFICATION RULE first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] The Recovery of the American Mind: Why Suicidal Empathy Is the Next Conservative Frontier
item.description:
It was a particular pleasure this week to welcome Gad Saad and his wife to Mississippi. Gad is one of the most important conservative thinkers writing today. His new book, Suicidal Empathy: Dying to Be Kind, is published next month, and I am the proud owner of a signed copy. Gad Saad's new book is […]

The post The Recovery of the American Mind: Why Suicidal Empathy Is the Next Conservative Frontier appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Teacher Appreciation Should Not Be Tied to Spending on Education
item.description: This week is Teacher Appreciation Week across the country, and it prompted Governor Brad Little to issue a press release where he had this to say: “This is Teacher Appreciation Week, a meaningful opportunity to recognize the people who stand at the front of our classrooms each day, shaping the future of our state. I […]

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] The Big, Beautiful Bill is law. Now Arkansas has to get it right.
item.description:
Passed almost one year ago, the famous “One Big Beautiful Bill” contains the most transformational welfare reforms in modern history. […]

The post The Big, Beautiful Bill is law. Now Arkansas has to get it right. first appeared on Opportunity Arkansas.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Cascade Quarterly Spring 2026
item.description:
Download the full PDF here

The post Cascade Quarterly Spring 2026 appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] DeLauro’s Energy Town Hall Became a Lesson in Coping
item.description:
Congresswoman Rosa DeLauro held a tele-town hall on April 6 to address rising energy costs and soaring gas prices. Connecticut residents called in with concerns about skyrocketing electric bills and […]

The post DeLauro’s Energy Town Hall Became a Lesson in Coping  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Empowerms] Josh McManus: Jackson’s Comeback Story
item.description:
Josh McManus: Jackson’s Comeback Story What does it take for a struggling city to turn the corner? In this episode of The Empower Podcast, Grant Callen sits down with urban strategist and entrepreneur Josh McManus to discuss the rise, decline, and revitalization of American cities, and why he believes Jackson still has enormous potential. Drawing…

The post Josh McManus: Jackson’s Comeback Story first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Dictionary Policing: What Omaha’s “Censored Shop” Tells Us About State Cartels
item.description:
If you’re in Omaha’s Blackstone District and you’re looking for a good cocktail, you might stumble into a tucked-away speakeasy now called the “Censored Shop.” Until recently, it went by “Barber Shop Blackstone.” According to the Nebraska Examiner, that name wasn’t a trick—it was a nod to the owners’ dad, who spent his life as...

The post Dictionary Policing: What Omaha’s “Censored Shop” Tells Us About State Cartels appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] The Myth of the Moral Vacuum: Why Every Law is a Moral Choice
item.description:
When the debate over vice laws, those governing drugs, gambling, or pornography, reaches the halls of our legislature, a familiar, hollow cry rings out: You can’t legislate morality! While it might be a catchy slogan (oft repeated by the vice peddlers themselves), it is designed to end a conversation before it begins. It suggests that […]

The post The Myth of the Moral Vacuum: Why Every Law is a Moral Choice appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] Venti-Sized Subsidy
item.description: Tennessee taxpayers are about to spend millions on a Starbucks order they didn’t ask for. In April, the world’s largest coffee chain announced it would be opening a Southeast corporate office in Music City.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Billionaires Are Not The Problem
item.description:
“You can’t earn a billion dollars. You just can’t earn that. You can get market power. You can break rules. You can do all sorts of things. You can abuse labor laws. You can pay people less than what they’re worth. But you can’t earn that.” These comments made by Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez on a […]

The post Billionaires Are Not The Problem appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Play for the Team on Your Jersey: It’s an Integrity Thing.
item.description: Imagine your favorite sports team. They all wear the same uniform, stand under the same banner, and operate under the same creed — not just to play, but to win according to a shared playbook. Each player brings unique strengths, but when they join the team, they all commit to the same rules, values, and […]

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] The Defeat of Measure 120 is a Win for the Oregon System
item.description:
By Naomi Inman It’s election time in Oregon and the Oregonian editorial board...

The post The Defeat of Measure 120 is a Win for the Oregon System appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Blog: Massachusetts Needs Growth—Not More of the Same
item.description:
Last week, the Massachusetts Taxpayers Foundation (MTF) released a “Bulletin” announcing its opposition to the tax cut ballot initiative. Odd for a taxpayers foundation to oppose a reduction in taxes. Even odder, however, was the Bulletin’s lack of concern about the Commonwealth’s current economic climate: dismal business formation, job losses, and weakening economic growth across the state. 

The post Blog: Massachusetts Needs Growth—Not More of the Same appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Horse Thieves Matter THE SON OF PIONEERS in the Flint Hills of Kansas, my sheriff grandfather was familiar with the story of “the Sedgwick County Anti-Horse-Thieving Association” — or the SCAHTA as it was never known because they were blessedly free of acronyms back then.  Let us just say that it would not have fit into the defund-the-police messaging, Read the full article...

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Governor Ron DeSantis Unveils Statue Honoring “Father of the Constitution” James Madison in Florida’s Madison County
item.description:
MADISON, Fla.—Today, Governor Ron DeSantis unveiled a new statue honoring President James Madison as part of Florida’s continued...

The post Governor Ron DeSantis Unveils Statue Honoring “Father of the Constitution” James Madison in Florida’s Madison County appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Great Nations Aren't Destroyed by Enemies. They're Destroyed by Debt.
item.description:
Imagine your family spent more this year than you earned. It wouldn't be comfortable, but you'd manage. Now imagine your family had spent more than it earned every single year since 2001. By now you'd be destitute. Well, that is exactly what the federal government has been doing. For the past 25 years — ever […]

The post Great Nations Aren't Destroyed by Enemies. They're Destroyed by Debt. appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Generational Fault Lines: Bronin’s Upset Win Over Larson Exposes Tensions Within Connecticut’s Labor Movement
item.description:
Former Hartford Mayor Luke Bronin surprised many Connecticut political observers by defeating Congressman John Larson for the Democratic convention endorsement in Connecticut’s 1st Congressional District, setting up an August primary […]

The post Generational Fault Lines: Bronin’s Upset Win Over Larson Exposes Tensions Within Connecticut’s Labor Movement  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] How NH can stop subsidizing out-of-state drivers
item.description:
The New Hampshire Turnpike System facilitates tourism and commuting in the Granite State. The three toll roads slash travel times in key corridors, which improves quality of life and helps the state’s economy grow. Turnpike users pay a small fee for the convenience of using these roads vs. more congested and slower local roads, essentially […]

The post How NH can stop subsidizing out-of-state drivers appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Manchester school board elevates educator staffing above all other priorities
item.description:
With Manchester’s school district looking to trim its proposed 2027 budget to stay within the city’s tax cap, the Board of School Committee took the extraordinary step of forbidding the district from achieving a balanced budget by laying off educators. The move effectively elevates raw educator staffing numbers over all other district priorities, even though […]

The post Manchester school board elevates educator staffing above all other priorities appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Beacontn] Beacon Applauds Department of Labor Decision to Withdraw Unlawful Overtime Rule
item.description: Today, the U.S. Department of Labor will formally publish a final rule overturning the Biden administration’s 2024 overtime rule after multiple federal courts vacated the rule nationwide and the department declined to continue defending it on appeal.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF talks to KOAT 7: What ever happened to taxpayers-subsidized Unser Crossing?
item.description:
New Mexico has a long history of spending taxpayer dollars on failed developments that don’t pan out. Going back nearly 20 years to the Unser Crossing development (originally slated for Unser and Central) was one such plan. Of course, RGF loves economic development. We want to see jobs and businesses locate to our state, but […]

The post RGF talks to KOAT 7: What ever happened to taxpayers-subsidized Unser Crossing? appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Franke: Memorial Day 2026
item.description: by Mark Frank Memorial Day is here again and much of our attention will be on those who died in service to their, our, country. As a popular meme says: All gave some. Some gave all. It is those who gave all that focus our memories now, while in November we thank everyone who served. Read the full article...

item.title: [Rifreedom] Unleashing Hope via K–12 Scholarships for Families: Why Rhode Island Should Opt-In to Federal Education Tax Credits
item.description:
Rhode Island students deserve better. The federal Education Freedom Tax Credit (EFTC) empowers working and middle-class families with scholarships for K-12 private schools, charters, homeschooling, tutoring & more at zero cost to RI taxpayers or public schools. The governor can opt-in today for January 2027 launch. Give Ocean State families real choice and brighter futures. Don’t leave millions in education funding on the table.

The post Unleashing Hope via K–12 Scholarships for Families: Why Rhode Island Should Opt-In to Federal Education Tax Credits appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] A Tale of Two Midwestern Cities: What Omaha Can Learn from Des Moines
item.description:
Note: This is the final blog post by Jakob Myers, the Platte Institute’s intern for the last year. Jakob has made great contributions to important policy areas for us since coming on board. This blog post is adapted from a research paper he wrote for one of his courses this spring and kicks off a...

The post A Tale of Two Midwestern Cities: What Omaha Can Learn from Des Moines appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Foropportunity] How Georgia’s Governor Impacts Your Daily Life: Understanding the Role and the Election Process
item.description:
From vetoing bills to deciding the budget, check out five ways Georgia’s governor impacts schools, safety, work, and public programs that our families and communities rely on.

The post How Georgia’s Governor Impacts Your Daily Life: Understanding the Role and the Election Process appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Freedom to Flourish Competes for Growth
item.description:
Iowa has pursued a fiscally conservative, growth-oriented strategy centered on lower taxes, restrained spending, and economic competitiveness under Governor Reynolds’ “Freedom to Flourish” philosophy. Recent reforms have strengthened the state’s fiscal position and improved its ability to compete for residents and investment. For Midwestern states like Iowa, long-term success will depend less on geography and more on affordability, fiscal stability, economic opportunity, and disciplined governance.

The post Freedom to Flourish Competes for Growth appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Empirecenter] New York Schools will Spend More and Deliver Less – Again
item.description:
As voters across New York head to the polls to decide their local school budgets, the big question – why....

The post New York Schools will Spend More and Deliver Less – Again appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] The Property Tax: Expensive and Outdated
item.description: When my dog gets hold of one of her toys, she won’t let it go. The harder I try to take it, the harder she holds on. I’m the same way about the revolutionary idea of eliminating property taxes in Idaho. The more you try to talk me out of abolishing these unfair, misguided, and […]

item.title: [Will Law] WILL’s America 250 Student Contest: ﻿“What America Means to Me”
item.description:
WILL announced the launch of its statewide student contest, “What America Means to Me,” celebrating the 250th anniversary of American Independence. The contest invites Wisconsin students in kindergarten through 12th grade to submit an original 30-60 second video reflecting on freedom, America’s founding principles, and the opportunities afforded by living in a free society.

The post WILL’s America 250 Student Contest: ﻿“What America Means to Me” appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Arkansaspolicyfoundation] SURPLUS REVENUES FUEL INCOME TAX CUTS
item.description: (May 2026) One sentence in an annual state report explains how policymakers have achieved a serious fiscal goal: reduction of the top state individual income tax rate under three governors and a succession of Republican legislatures. “(Arkansas) revenues are expanding because the business cycle’s current state is expansion, not contraction.” Policy Foundation research memo, Nov. […]

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Iowa Moved to Reduce Barriers to Rural Health Care
item.description:
Research comparing states with and without Certificate of Need (CON) laws suggests CON requirements can limit competition and reduce access to rural health care services. During the 2026 legislative session, Iowa passed significant CON reforms that reduce regulatory barriers and narrow the number of projects requiring state approval. The reforms should make it easier for providers to expand services, encourage investment in underserved areas, and improve health care access for Iowa patients and communities.

The post Iowa Moved to Reduce Barriers to Rural Health Care appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Pioneer Institute Study Calls for Attrition Strategy to Rein in Record-High Massachusetts State Workforce
item.description:
BOSTON – The size of the state workforce reached an all-time high in 2025, and a new Pioneer Institute study recommends implementing an attrition program that would reduce the number of positions under the governor’s authority by 5,000 and save taxpayers $1.5 billion over five years by backfilling three of every four positions that are vacated.

The post Pioneer Institute Study Calls for Attrition Strategy to Rein in Record-High Massachusetts State Workforce appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] The Gig Is Up for the 2024 Independent Contractor Rule
item.description:
WILL submitted a comment letter to the U.S. Department of Labor in support of a reversing a Biden-era independent contractor rule which actively threatened the independence of workers and businesses by trapping them in an unpredictable regulatory guessing game.

The post The Gig Is Up for the 2024 Independent Contractor Rule appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Oklahoma farmers benefit from an assessment limit
item.description: Perhaps the strongest objection to an assessment limit over the last two years came from the Kansas Farm Bureau, which was concerned that a limit “could potentially reduce property taxes for certain classes of property on certain years when the appraised value of that property exceeds 3%” and prompt a shift in the tax burden. […]

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Allow more ADUs to boost housing supply and comply with state law
item.description:
May 20, 2026, 9 a.m. Kalana O Maui Building To: Maui County Council House and Land Use Committee Nohelani U‘u-Hodgins, Chair Kauanoe Batangan, Vice Chair From: Jonathan Helton, Policy Analyst Grassroot Institute of Hawaii RE: Bill 78 (2026) — ON ACCESSORY DWELLINGS ON RESIDENTIALLY ZONED LOTS Aloha Chair U‘u-Hodgins, Vice Chair Bantangan and other members […]

The post Allow more ADUs to boost housing supply and comply with state law appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Arkansaspolicyfoundation] TOP ARKANSAS INCOME TAX RATE CUT TO 3.7%
item.description: (May 2026) Arkansas policymakers reduced the top state individual income tax rate from 3.9% to 3.7% in early May, using an estimated $334.4 million projected fiscal surplus in the current 2025-26 fiscal year. Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders called a special session of the state legislature April 30 to consider the tax cut. The session convened […]

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Who Controls Your Electric Bill? Louisiana Voters Are Deciding Right Now.
item.description: The post Who Controls Your Electric Bill? Louisiana Voters Are Deciding Right Now. appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH offers the lowest gas and grocery prices in New England this Memorial Day Weekend
item.description:
Granite Staters can expect to have the most affordable Memorial Day Weekend in New England this year, as New Hampshire offers the region’s lowest gas and grocery prices, a Josiah Bartlett Center for Public Policy review of regional prices shows.  Memorial Day weekend kicks off both summer travel season and outdoor grilling season, and this […]

The post NH offers the lowest gas and grocery prices in New England this Memorial Day Weekend appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Building on the Wisconsin higher-ed reform model
item.description:
Wisconsin can pursue practical, targeted reforms that improve accountability, strengthen academic standards and better align universities with the needs of students and taxpayers without dismantling the university system itself.

The post Building on the Wisconsin higher-ed reform model appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Budget Deal Reportedly Earmarks $100M for 1199 and Extends MCO Tax
item.description:
As Governor Hochul and legislative leaders rush to finalize the overdue state budget, outlines of some healthcare-related deals have begun....

The post Budget Deal Reportedly Earmarks $100M for 1199 and Extends MCO Tax appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Here we go again; JoCo districts prepare another school funding lawsuit rather than educating students
item.description: When the Kansas Supreme Court ruled in 2019 that school funding met its definition of constitutionally adequate, we wrote that it was only a matter of time before the next school funding lawsuit. Seven years later, four Johnson County school districts (Blue Valley, De Soto, Olathe, and Shawnee Mission) announced they are preparing to do […]

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] New York Says “Yes” to Scholarships–Oregon Should Too
item.description:
By Kathryn Hickok Governor Kathy Hochul announced May 5 that she intends to...

The post New York Says “Yes” to Scholarships–Oregon Should Too appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] CTDOL Finally Enforces the Union Transparency Law It Tried to Kill
item.description:
Connecticut’s Department of Labor (CTDOL) has discovered a novel approach to state law: complying with it.  On May 13, Labor Commissioner Danté Bartolomeo emailed the co-chairs and ranking members of […]

The post CTDOL Finally Enforces the Union Transparency Law It Tried to Kill appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Gas Gimmicks Won’t Cut Prices
item.description:
Louisiana families feel fuel prices before politicians finish explaining them. Gasoline is the cost of getting to work, hauling equipment, running boats, moving freight through ports, taking kids to school, and keeping small businesses alive. As of May 22, AAA’s Louisiana gas price data showed regular gasoline averaging $4.051 per gallon, compared with a national average...

The post Gas Gimmicks Won’t Cut Prices appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] LPSC Explores New Electricity Options
item.description:
Louisiana regulators have officially opened the door to one of the most consequential energy policy debates in decades. At the April meeting of the Louisiana Public Service Commission, Commissioner JP Coussan directed staff to open a rulemaking docket evaluating “private use electrical networks” — a move that could fundamentally reshape how large-scale energy projects are...

The post LPSC Explores New Electricity Options appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Memorial Day: Bravery and Sacrifice
item.description:
From all of us at the Show-Me Institute, thank you to all the men and women who have served and continue to serve our great nation. We will never forget […]

The post Memorial Day: Bravery and Sacrifice appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] A good lesson in curtailing government growth
item.description:
Do not let it be said that I only criticize government officials. I also like to give credit where credit is due. This week, a substantial amount of credit and praise is owed to the Honolulu Budget Committee, which made the fiscally responsible decision to ax most of the funding for the city’s Office of […]

The post A good lesson in curtailing government growth appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Mackinac] Michigan shows how we can make homeownership affordable
item.description: Reform state and local zoning laws

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] More Americans are forced to pay Obamacare taxes
item.description: Multiple attempts at repealing Obamacare entirely have been unsuccessful. However, Americans would welcome common-sense reforms that would guarantee the program’s effectiveness and its financial future while reducing the growing tax burden on Americans.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Tallahassee Democrat: Levy Cap: Florida’s Smartest Option for Property Tax Reform
item.description:
Florida needs property tax reform that is sustainable and honest about trade-offs. Of the options now under consideration,...

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] New Report: Arizona’s Past Is Being Buried by Political Correctness
item.description:
Arizona has long been a hub of archaeological discovery, helping scholars uncover the history of the ancient Southwest. But a..

The post New Report: Arizona’s Past Is Being Buried by Political Correctness appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Believe it or not, Washington Once Had Fiscal Restraint
item.description:
A century ago, Presidents Warren G. Harding and Calvin Coolidge made balancing budgets, reducing debt, and limiting government growth national priorities. Their administrations did not just slow spending growth — they actually cut federal spending, reduced the national debt, lowered tax rates, and produced budget surpluses. Fiscal restraint was politically difficult then, just as it is today, but the Harding and Coolidge years demonstrate that disciplined budgeting and strong economic growth can coexist.

The post Believe it or not, Washington Once Had Fiscal Restraint appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Every so often, Big Labor lets its mask slip
item.description:
A union executive flipped us off through an office window in Wilmington, Del., after we delivered information explaining workers’ constitutional rights.

The post Every so often, Big Labor lets its mask slip appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Empowerms] Rep. Otis Anthony: Can the Delta Rise Again?
item.description:
Rep. Otis Anthony: Can the Delta Rise Again? On this episode of the Empower Podcast, State Rep. Otis Anthony joins Grant for a wide-ranging conversation about poverty, work, education, economic development, and the future of the Mississippi Delta. Anthony shares how growing up in Indianola shaped his view of public service, why he believes the Delta’s…

The post Rep. Otis Anthony: Can the Delta Rise Again? first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] AI and the Future of College with Jacob Light
item.description:
Susan Pendergrass speaks with Jacob Light, Hoover Fellow at the Hoover Institution, about his research on how artificial intelligence is reshaping higher education. They explore which college majors are most […]

The post AI and the Future of College with Jacob Light appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Missouri Students Continue to Fall Behind
item.description:
For years, the education establishment in Missouri has relied on a predictable playbook. Whenever state test scores drop or national rankings look bleak, we are told that the data don’t […]

The post Missouri Students Continue to Fall Behind appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] California’s ‘Scarcity Mindset’
item.description:
The late, great comedian Sam Kinison once said that instead of sending food to starving nations, we should send U-Hauls because, he would scream, “there wouldn’t be world hunger if you people would live where the food is! You live in a desert, understand that?! Nothing grows out of here!” It’s a classic bit, but […]

The post California’s ‘Scarcity Mindset’ appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Libertas] Libertas Network Acquires Family Brand to Strengthen Intentional Families
item.description:
Libertas Network’s acquisition of Praxis ignites a bold revolution in education by empowering young innovators to bypass outdated systems and chart their own path to success.

The post Libertas Network Acquires Family Brand to Strengthen Intentional Families appeared first on Libertas Network: Changing Hearts, Minds, and Laws.

item.title: [Mackinac] It’s time to shut down the MEDC
item.description: There are better ways to foster economic growth

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG Analysis: New DPI Report Shows Wisconsin Schools Break All-Time Staffing Record
item.description:
For Immediate Release May 27, 2026 Contact: press@reforminggovernment.org   IRG Analysis: New DPI Report Shows Wisconsin Schools Break All-Time Staffing Record More referenda likely as schools employ the most staff per student in state history   Delafield, Wis. –  Wisconsin public schools now employ 1 adult for every 7 students, breaking the all-time record from […]

The post IRG Analysis: New DPI Report Shows Wisconsin Schools Break All-Time Staffing Record appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] The runup to the Declaration of Independence
item.description: Americans knew of the long struggle for liberty waged against the Crown by their British ancestors. Absolute submission to a government in which they had no representation was not a condition they were prepared to accept.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansas Should Welcome AI Growth, Not Zone It Away
item.description: Artificial intelligence is not floating in the clouds. It runs on land, energy, fiber, water systems, skilled workers, and data centers. That means Kansas has a choice. It can become a place where the next generation of digital infrastructure is built, or it can regulate, zone, and subsidize its way into mediocrity while investment goes […]

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Pioneer Institute Applauds Healey Administration’s Proposal to Reduce LLC Filing Fees; More Reforms Needed to Address Economic Malaise
item.description:
Pioneer Institute applauds the Healey Administration’s proposal to reduce the LLC filing fee from $500 to $100 and discount annual LLC fees during the early years of a new business. These changes remove two unnecessary barriers facing entrepreneurs and small business owners...

The post Pioneer Institute Applauds Healey Administration’s Proposal to Reduce LLC Filing Fees; More Reforms Needed to Address Economic Malaise appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Attorney General Mike Hilgers on How We Grow Nebraska
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Nebraska’s Attorney General, Mike Hilgers. During the episode Attorney General Hilgers and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss the the importance of good energy policy in Nebraska, how we attract new residents and investment, and the next big issues that will shape the future of...

The post Nebraskanomics: Attorney General Mike Hilgers on How We Grow Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] CalPERS Pays Million-Dollar Salaries for Below-Median Returns
item.description:
The California Public Employees’ Retirement System (CalPERS) continues to demonstrate a stunning disconnect between its investment performance and the compensation it awards its top administrators. A 255-page forensic investigation conducted by former SEC lawyer Edward Siedle and pension analyst Chris Tobe, commissioned by the Retired Public Employees’ Association of California, details a culture of secrecy...

The post CalPERS Pays Million-Dollar Salaries for Below-Median Returns first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Backs HUD Plan to Let Public Housing Add Work Requirements
item.description:
WILL filed a public comment in support of a new Housing and Urban Development (HUD) rule that would let public housing agencies and landlords require able-bodied adults receiving housing assistance to work up to 40 hours per week and limit assistance to two years.

The post WILL Backs HUD Plan to Let Public Housing Add Work Requirements appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] New website empowers public employees to challenge corporate unions
item.description:
A project of the Freedom Foundation, Empowered-Employees.com launched today to help public servants understand their options when it comes to workplace representation.

The post New website empowers public employees to challenge corporate unions appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Fine Tuning the “Water Renaissance” Plan
item.description:
The recently released “Water Renaissance” plan, a product of “conservation groups and tribes,” gets a very big idea right. There is no reason why California’s coastal megacities should have to import water. With that one visionary presumption, this report has made a major contribution. In fact, it doesn’t go far enough. With massive, targeted investments,...

The post Fine Tuning the “Water Renaissance” Plan first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Proclaiming Liberty — 6/22 Virtual Event
item.description:
As America celebrates the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence, join the Goldwater Institute for a special webinar discussion..

The post Proclaiming Liberty — 6/22 Virtual Event appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] When City Councils Play Secretary of State
item.description:
In the years since the National Civic League published “Is It None of Our Business?,” the trend of city halls tackling national issues has shifted from a novelty to a standard operating model. The League’s 2023 analysis argued that local agencies should embrace a broader national agenda—a shift they championed as a positive phenomenon. However, from...

The post When City Councils Play Secretary of State first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Mark Belling discusses IRG’s analysis regarding the new DPI report showing that Wisconsin schools have broken an all-time staffing record
item.description:
Mark Belling discusses IRG’s analysis regarding the new DPI report showing that Wisconsin schools have broken an all-time staffing record Listen here.  

The post Mark Belling discusses IRG’s analysis regarding the new DPI report showing that Wisconsin schools have broken an all-time staffing record appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Next Round’s 2026 California Primary Preview
item.description:
This week, Tim previews next week’s California primary election.  He takes a deep dive on the governor’s race and explores whether Xavier Becerra has unstoppable momentum to making the November election, and whether two Democrats may shut out the Republican contenders for the fall campaign.  He also discusses the wild race for Los Angeles mayor, […]

The post Next Round’s 2026 California Primary Preview appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Minnesota’s overreliance on shaky financing schemes threatens HCMC amid CMS crackdown
item.description:
But once the billions in new federal money began flowing in, the idea of cutting the sick tax was abandoned. Even Republican “fiscal hawks” couldn’t resist keeping the tax in place, and the federal money that was supposed to replace it disappeared into Minnesota’s exploding state budget — now a national punchline.

The post Minnesota’s overreliance on shaky financing schemes threatens HCMC amid CMS crackdown appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Property tax rollback bill should protect against unjust penalties
item.description:
May 27, 2026, 8:30 a.m. Historic County Building To: Kaua'i County Council Arryl Kaneshiro, Chair KipuKai Kuali'i, Vice Chair From: Grassroot Institute of Hawaii Jonathan Helton, Policy Analyst RE: Bill 2995 — RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAX Aloha Chair Kaneshiro, Vice Chair Kuali'i and other Committee members, The Grassroot Institute of Hawaii offers comments on — […]

The post Property tax rollback bill should protect against unjust penalties appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Left-wing group targets standardized tests in Houston
item.description:
Jessica Costescu and Ethan Barton write for the Washington Free Beacon about education mischief in the Lone Star State. A Houston-based education advocacy group fighting to eliminate standardized test requirements is led by a white left-wing activist who accused the Texas government of racism after it obeyed a state law written by a black Democrat...

The post Left-wing group targets standardized tests in Houston appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Tax dollars helped SPLC fund race-baiting programs
item.description:
Breccan Thies writes for the Federalist about a disturbing revelation involving a prominent left-wing organization. The far-left Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC), currently under federal indictment for allegedly funding hate hoaxes, has been able to push its propaganda in schools through millions of dollars in direct payments from government entities at all levels. According to...

The post Tax dollars helped SPLC fund race-baiting programs appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Mamdani wants to seize, redistribute private property
item.description:
Dmitri Bolt writes for Townhall.com about the New York City mayor following through on his socialist ideology. New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani kicked off his plan to “fix” the city’s affordability crisis with his official housing policy agenda, titled “Block by Block,” under which one of the first priorities is to allow the government...

The post Mamdani wants to seize, redistribute private property appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] School boards should represent the public, not silence it
item.description: Ominous trend toward secrecy and intolerance for opposition

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Illinois budget proposes $200,000 to redefine ‘low income’
item.description:
The proposed state budget would pay to develop a new definition in the face of federal changes to the SNAP program.

The post Illinois budget proposes $200,000 to redefine ‘low income’ appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Benjamin Yount Show: May 28, 2026
item.description:
Benjamin Yount discusses new IRG analysis on DPI.  

The post Benjamin Yount Show: May 28, 2026 appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: ‘Affordable’ Housing “A Conservative is a fellow who is standing athwart history yelling ‘Stop,’ at a time when no one is inclined to do so.” — Mission Statement, the National Review READING UP on the Indianapolis “Vacant to Vibrant” housing plan, I’m honestly having trouble differentiating it from Zohran Mamdani’s New York City.  Both assume that “affordable” housing Read the full article...

item.title: [Texaspolicy] AI is Changing Higher Education
item.description:
The AI boom of the past few years has rendered an inevitable reaction. Technology that is supposedly capable of replacing much of the cognitive tasks of human beings has shaken higher education to its core. The hype enjoyed by this fundamental shift in thinking has remained a fascination of our great minds of the Western...

The post AI is Changing Higher Education first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Good riddance to non-use of public lands
item.description: The rule rescission brings common sense back to federal land management while also protecting the livelihoods of thousands who responsibly make a living on our shared inheritance.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Albuquerque’s fumble on modest upzoning to promote housing
item.description:
With a projected housing need of 55,000 new units by 2045, the city has passed some minor housing reforms in recent years, including legalizing accessory dwelling units and allowing more housing near major transit lines. But the city has been reluctant to undertake citywide zoning reforms, despite the city’s housing needs assessment recommending doing so. […]

The post Albuquerque’s fumble on modest upzoning to promote housing appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Pritzker’s proposed budget shorts pensions by $5.4 billion
item.description:
Actuaries say Illinois needs to put in $17 billion a year to fix the plans, but the 2027 plan calls for far less.

The post Pritzker’s proposed budget shorts pensions by $5.4 billion appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] CALIFORNIA: IHSS providers tricked into staying, overpaying
item.description:
We hear it all too often from In-Home Support Services (IHSS) care providers considering opting out of their union: “If I do, I’ll lose my dental insurance.”

The post CALIFORNIA: IHSS providers tricked into staying, overpaying appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] AICOA Would Pull Up the Ladder on Florida’s Small Businesses
item.description:
Washington didn’t build Florida’s 3.5 million small businesses. Low taxes, light regulation, and an entrepreneurial culture built an economy where 3.8 million Floridians...

The post AICOA Would Pull Up the Ladder on Florida’s Small Businesses appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Why the Left Should Celebrate Supreme Court Decision Ending Racial Gerrymandering
item.description:
Democrats and the progressive left continue to lambast the Supreme Court’s 6-3 decision in Louisiana v. Callais to strike down the Bayou State’s second majority-Black congressional district as a racial gerrymander. Democrat leaders have used vicious and ugly attacks to pronounce the decision as racist and insist that it guts the Voting Rights Act (VRA). In...

The post Why the Left Should Celebrate Supreme Court Decision Ending Racial Gerrymandering first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Mspolicy] UPSKILL: Putting the state's needs first, avoiding big questions
item.description:
Lawmakers across the country often insist on making “investments” in economic programs to help fill a perceived need for skilled workers. But such efforts often put the needs of the state and favored industries ahead of the needs of individuals -- and they miss big picture questions in the process.  This critical view should be […]

The post UPSKILL: Putting the state's needs first, avoiding big questions appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Pritzker budget gives NASCAR, PGA up to $9 million in taxpayer money
item.description:
The proposed Illinois budget gives money to NASCAR and PGA as lawmakers weigh a Bears stadium tax break, putting taxpayers on the hook for private sports costs.

The post Pritzker budget gives NASCAR, PGA up to $9 million in taxpayer money appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Lack of Common Sense on Energy in the Budget
item.description:
Lack of Common Sense on Energy in the Budget Anyone hoping the governor would make even modest, common-sense changes to....

The post Lack of Common Sense on Energy in the Budget appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] TPPF Unveils Texas Election Integrity Agenda Ahead of 90th Legislative Session
item.description:
AUSTIN – The Texas Public Policy Foundation today unveiled its Texas Election Integrity Agenda ahead of the 90th Legislative Session, outlining a legislative agenda aimed at strengthening election administration and ensuring Texans continue to trust in the state’s voting process. “Protecting the integrity of Texas elections requires strong ballot security, proof-of-citizenship safeguards, and clear protections against...

The post TPPF Unveils Texas Election Integrity Agenda Ahead of 90th Legislative Session first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] BAC 0.364
item.description:
Hugo Cardona-Jiminez will be returning to his native Guatemala on his third deportation following a hearing this morning at the federal courthouse in downtown St. Paul. Your correspondent was there. Cardona, now aged 31, entered a guilty plea to a federal felony criminal charge of illegal re-entry of a previously deported alien. Court records show...

The post BAC 0.364 appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: SPEED Act would be a win for Left and Right
item.description:
The following appeared in the Eastern New Mexico News on May 5, 2026, and in several other New Mexico media outlets. Federal legislation known as the Standardizing Permitting and Expediting Economic Development (SPEED Act) would make needed improvements to America’s antiquated federal permitting system for energy projects. While Sen. Martin Heinrich and the Rio Grande […]

The post Opinion piece: SPEED Act would be a win for Left and Right appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Inspection vs. Intimidation from the USDA
item.description:
There is a particular unease that sets in when someone with government authority walks into your business and begins issuing..

The post Inspection vs. Intimidation from the USDA appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Wisconsin eventually will opt in to donor bonanza for schools, business leader predicts
item.description:
“There’s never been a federal program that’s been as generous as this that the state didn’t opt in,” said Kooyenga.

The post Wisconsin eventually will opt in to donor bonanza for schools, business leader predicts appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Restoring accountability in Wisconsin government
item.description:
Wisconsin’ recent failures of accountability share a common feature: Power has increasingly shifted away from the Legislature and toward governors, agencies, courts and other institutions that operate further from direct democratic control.

The post Restoring accountability in Wisconsin government appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] For smaller businesses, Minnesota has the highest effective corporate tax rate in the United States
item.description:
Back in January, the Tax Foundation published their ranking of state corporate income taxes for 2026. As Figure 1 shows, with a corporate income tax rate of 9.8%, Minnesota ranked second after only New Jersey with its rate of 11.5%. Figure 1 But New Jersey only imposes that 11.5% rate on net taxable income above...

The post For smaller businesses, Minnesota has the highest effective corporate tax rate in the United States appeared first on American Experiment.
